title: festuca ovina subvar. eubriquetii
intro:
Festuca ovina subvar. eubriquetii, commonly known as sheep's fescue, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The therapeutic actions of Festuca ovina subvar. eubriquetii include bronchodilation, reducing inflammation, and improving respiratory function. Its bioactive constituents include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

Note: The plant's properties, health benefits, and bioactive constituents might not be fully researched or documented, so this information may not be comprehensive or accurate.

side_effects: Festuca ovina subvar. eubriquetii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal irritation, nausea, and vomiting due to the plant's high concentration of saponins and phenolic compounds. Ingestion of the plant can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and respiratory issues in sensitive individuals. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils has been linked to headaches, dizziness, and confusion. If not handled correctly, Festuca ovina subvar. eubriquetii can also cause skin and eye irritation, exacerbating existing conditions like eczema or conjunctivitis.
